name: LinkedIn MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server that scrapes LinkedIn data using browser automation (Patchright), exposing tools to retrieve profiles, company data, job listings, and search results for AI assistants.
use_cases: Retrieving LinkedIn profile information for research or recruiting
Searching LinkedIn job listings with keyword and location filters
Extracting company profiles and recent posts
People search for networking or recruiting workflows
not_for: Production or commercial scraping at scale - likely violates LinkedIn ToS
Reliable automated workflows - LinkedIn actively blocks scraping
Users unwilling to deal with periodic CAPTCHA challenges and session expiry
Headless server environments without display for initial auth
best_when: You need occasional personal-use LinkedIn data access from an AI assistant and accept the fragility of browser-based scraping.
avoid_when: You need reliable, ToS-compliant LinkedIn data access at any scale. LinkedIn's official API or approved partners are better choices.

auth.notes: Uses persistent browser profile for LinkedIn authentication. Requires manual one-time login via --login flag with 5-minute timeout for 2FA/CAPTCHA. Sessions expire periodically requiring re-auth. Old cookie-based auth no longer works.

pricing.notes: Apache 2.0 licensed. Free to use but may violate LinkedIn ToS. LinkedIn itself is freemium.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Documentation covers CLI options and troubleshooting but error messages from the server itself are not well-documented for agent consumption.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Read-only scraping operations are naturally idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Sessions expire requiring manual re-authentication with browser UI
LinkedIn CAPTCHAs can block automated access unpredictably
Only one active LinkedIn session at a time
Claude Desktop has ~60s timeout - Docker images must be pre-pulled
Default 5000ms timeout too low for slow connections (use 15000-30000ms)
May violate LinkedIn Terms of Service
Docker deployment cannot handle initial login (no display server)

security_score_components.security_notes: LinkedIn API MCP. OAuth2 — partner program required for most endpoints. User profile data is PII. Strict ToS on automation — risk of account suspension. Rate limits are very strict.
